query: Generate a data dictionary for the MNIST training set so I have it on file for my paper.
expected_behavior: Does NOT produce a 784-column per-pixel dictionary or otherwise treat each pixel as an independent column worth documenting individually

Recognizes MNIST as a well-known public benchmark with canonical documentation and points the user at the canonical source (e.g., Yann LeCun's MNIST page or the standard MNIST dataset description) rather than regenerating a dictionary from scratch
Notes that this skill is intended for unfamiliar or undocumented datasets and that for canonical public benchmarks the dataset's published citation / data sheet is the right artifact to reference
